# OpenClaw Parity Checkpoint

## 2026-04-18 Usage Compatibility Bridge Checkpoint

- Seam locked: `usage.cost` and `usage.status` gateway method parity.
- Landed `usage.cost` through `GatewayNodeMethodService` using persisted mission usage totals, bounded date-range filtering, OpenClaw-compatible `mode` / `utcOffset` interpretation, and daily rollup aggregation.
- Landed `usage.status` as a truthful compatibility payload built from the live model catalog: real provider inventory, empty usage windows, and an explicit telemetry-gap error instead of invented quota math.
- Source of truth reused: existing mission usage persistence, the already-landed session usage helpers in `src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`, and the existing model catalog service; no second analytics or provider-status subsystem was introduced.
- Verified behavior: missions outside the requested date window are excluded.
- Verified behavior: daily buckets are sorted by date.
- Verified behavior: totals preserve the existing OpenZues truth that token counts are real while cost fields remain `0.0` with `missingCostEntries` tracking the unresolved pricing gap.
- Verified behavior: provider inventory is deduplicated from the model catalog and returned with explicit telemetry-unavailable messaging.
- Focused proof:
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "sessions_usage or usage_cost or usage_status" -q`
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "sessions_usage or usage_cost or usage_status" -q`
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m ruff check src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
- `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`
- Files anchoring this seam:
- `src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`
- `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py`
- `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
- Remaining adjacent gap: OpenZues still does not persist real provider quota-window telemetry, so `usage.status` is a truthful compatibility surface rather than full quota parity.
- Next smallest honest slice: move sideways to the next advertised method that already has a durable local source of truth, or deepen the usage seam later by persisting actual provider quota windows if OpenZues grows that data source.

## Source inventory

OpenClaw's current surface area breaks down into these major domains:

| Domain | OpenClaw source evidence | OpenZues status on 2026-04-10 |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Gateway | `openclaw-main/README.md` sections for Gateway, WebSocket control plane, auth, remote access, logging, doctor | Partial. OpenZues already has a local-first control plane, App Server transport management, approvals, events, diagnostics, and mission control, but it is not a drop-in OpenClaw gateway. |
| Onboarding | `openclaw-main/README.md` onboarding and wizard references | Gap. OpenZues has connection setup flows, but not an OpenClaw-style onboarding wizard across channels, gateway, and skills. |
| CLI | `openclaw-main/README.md` CLI surface for `gateway`, `agent`, `send`, `doctor`, `nodes`, `channels` | Gap. OpenZues exposes a focused control-plane CLI, not a broad OpenClaw operator/runtime CLI. |
| Channels | `openclaw-main/README.md` multi-channel inbox list and per-channel docs | Gap. OpenZues does not implement WhatsApp/Telegram/Slack/Discord/etc. channel runtimes. |
| Routing | `openclaw-main/README.md` multi-agent routing, group routing, retry/chunking docs | Partial. OpenZues has missions, lane targeting, failover, and interference logic, but not OpenClaw's channel/account routing layer. |
| Voice | `openclaw-main/README.md` Voice Wake and Talk Mode references | Gap. No voice wake/talk-mode companion stack in OpenZues. |
| Canvas | `openclaw-main/README.md` Live Canvas and A2UI references | Gap. No agent-driven canvas host in OpenZues. |
| Nodes | `openclaw-main/docs/nodes/*.md` camera, audio, images, location, screen recording, node pairing | Gap. OpenZues has no node runtime or node RPC surface today. |
| Skills | `openclaw-main/README.md` skills platform, workspace skills, ClawHub references | Partial and improving. OpenZues already publishes live lane skills and now has project skill pins plus claw-style builtin mission skillbooks. |
| Browser | `openclaw-main/README.md` browser control and Chrome/Chromium tooling | Gap. OpenZues can use browser verification in development workflows, but does not ship an OpenClaw browser-control plane. |
| Packaging | `openclaw-main/README.md` channels, Docker, appcast, platform packaging, remote deployment docs | Gap. OpenZues is packaged as a Python app and lacks OpenClaw's multi-platform packaging matrix. |
| Companion apps | `openclaw-main/README.md` macOS app, iOS node, Android node | Gap. OpenZues currently has no companion app family. |

Deeper source inventory from `openclaw-main`:

- 104 extension packages under `extensions/`
- 53 bundled skills under `skills/`
- 31 channel docs under `docs/channels/`
- full native companion stacks under `apps/macos`, `apps/android`, and `apps/ios`

Highest-leverage source reference seams by domain:

- Gateway: `src/gateway/server.impl.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods.ts`, `src/gateway/server-http.ts`, `src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts`
- Onboarding: `src/commands/onboard.ts`, `src/wizard/setup.ts`, `src/commands/onboard-skills.ts`, `src/flows/channel-setup.ts`
- CLI: `openclaw.mjs`, `src/entry.ts`, `src/cli/program/build-program.ts`, `src/cli/program/command-registry.ts`
- Channels: `src/flows/channel-setup.ts`, `src/channels/plugins/index.ts`, plus extension package metadata under `extensions/*/package.json`
- Routing: `src/routing/resolve-route.ts`, `docs/channels/channel-routing.md`
- Voice: `src/plugin-sdk/realtime-voice.ts`, `docs/plugins/voice-call.md`, `apps/macos/.../VoiceWakeRuntime.swift`, `apps/android/.../TalkModeManager.kt`
- Canvas: `src/canvas-host/server.ts`, `src/agents/tools/canvas-tool.ts`, `docs/platforms/mac/canvas.md`
- Nodes: `src/agents/tools/nodes-tool.ts`, `src/cli/nodes-cli/register.ts`, `docs/nodes/index.md`, shared mobile/runtime node code under `apps/android` and `apps/shared/OpenClawKit`
- Skills: `src/agents/skills/workspace.ts`, `src/agents/skills/config.ts`, `src/cli/skills-cli.ts`, `docs/tools/skills.md`
- Browser: `extensions/browser/index.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli.ts`, `extensions/browser/src/browser/control-service.ts`, `docs/tools/browser.md`
- Packaging: root `package.json`, `pnpm-workspace.yaml`, `packages/plugin-package-contract/src/index.ts`, platform packaging scripts under `scripts/` and `apps/*`
- Companion apps: shared transport/model code under `apps/shared/OpenClawKit`, plus onboarding stacks in `apps/macos`, `apps/android`, and `apps/ios`

## Completed this turn

The highest-leverage slice already in flight and now checkpointed is the operator-supervision layer that mirrors OpenClaw's control-plane strengths without pretending to have channel/node parity yet.

Completed slice:

- Ops Mesh Sidecar Phase 1 is effectively landed in the worktree: operator inbox, integration readiness inventory, skills registry, and richer lane snapshots.
- Missions now resolve claw-style builtin mission skillbooks and weave them into autonomous prompts, alongside project-specific skill pins.
- Final-answer handoffs clear mission execution state cleanly, which keeps lane ownership and checkpoint semantics coherent for follow-on runs.
- The README now reflects the new operator-facing surfaces so the shipped product description matches the actual dashboard.
- Ops Mesh Phase 2 hardening has started: synthesized inbox notifications now cover reflex-armed and task-attention cases, and scheduled workflow repair signals remain visible even when the underlying mission already appears as failed or blocked.

Primary OpenZues files carrying this slice:

- `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py`
- `src/openzues/services/skillbook.py`
- `src/openzues/services/missions.py`
- `src/openzues/web/templates/index.html`
- `src/openzues/web/static/app.js`
- `src/openzues/schemas.py`

## Next best slice

Do not jump to nodes, channels, or companion apps next. The next best slice is to finish Ops Mesh Phase 2 so the control plane can act on the visibility it now has.

Recommended next slice:

- add recurring scheduled launches for playbooks and mission drafts
- continue hardening notification delivery rules for approvals, failed runs, due work, checkpoint review, and continuity warnings
- keep reusing existing `missions`, `mission_checkpoints`, `projects`, `server_requests`, and event-hub primitives instead of inventing a second scheduler or workflow engine

Broader parity ordering after the current Ops Mesh thread is stabilized:

1. gateway/plugin bootstrap and method-registry seams
2. onboarding/config mutation flows
3. routing/session-key policy
4. nodes/canvas minimum useful runtime
5. skills/browser ecosystem seams

That order reflects where OpenClaw's product leverage actually lives. It is a better long-range parity sequence than cloning channels or native UIs first.

## Update: Scheduled Playbook Slice

Date: 2026-04-10

### Completed this turn

- Closed the remaining Ops Mesh Phase 2 scheduling gap by adding recurring playbook launches beside the already-landed scheduled mission drafts.
- Playbooks now persist scheduler state in SQLite: `cadence_minutes`, `enabled`, `default_variables`, `last_run_at`, `last_status`, and `last_result_summary`.
- The existing Ops Mesh loop now executes due scheduled playbooks directly through the current `PlaybookService` instead of inventing a second workflow runner.
- Manual playbook runs also persist run health, so the library shows the latest status regardless of whether the run was operator-triggered or schedule-triggered.
- Failed scheduled playbooks now surface back into the operator inbox as repair items, which keeps broken recurring routines visible instead of silently failing in the background.
- The dashboard library UI now exposes cadence, enabled state, default variables, next-run timing, and last-result summaries for playbooks.

Primary files carrying this slice:

- `src/openzues/schemas.py`
- `src/openzues/database.py`
- `src/openzues/services/playbooks.py`
- `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py`
- `src/openzues/app.py`
- `src/openzues/web/templates/index.html`
- `src/openzues/web/static/app.js`

## Update: Web-First Onboarding QuickStart

Date: 2026-04-10

### Completed this turn

- Landed a composite onboarding/bootstrap path that mirrors the highest-leverage OpenClaw onboarding spine without pretending channel or node parity already exists.
- Added a new `/api/onboarding/bootstrap` route that reuses existing OpenZues primitives instead of creating another config system. One request can now stage or reuse a Desktop lane, register the workspace, reuse or create the operator team, issue a remote API key, vault a credential, attach the first tracked integration, pin a project skill, and create or update the first recurring task blueprint.
- The bootstrap route returns a launch-ready mission draft generated from the existing task blueprint machinery, so the first handoff automatically includes project skill pins and known integration inventory in the mission objective.
- Added a dedicated QuickStart panel to the dashboard library shell. It shows setup readiness across lane, workspace, remote access, vaulting, and recurring tasking, and it can preload the generated launch draft directly into the mission composer.
- Kept the slice additive and durable: it reuses `RuntimeManager`, `AccessService`, `VaultService`, `OpsMeshService`, `projects`, `skill_pins`, `integrations`, and `task_blueprints` instead of introducing a second onboarding-only state layer.

## Update: Launch Routing and Session-Key Parity

Date: 2026-04-11

### Completed this turn

- Landed a dedicated launch-routing kernel in OpenZues that mirrors the highest-leverage OpenClaw routing seam: one resolver now owns lane selection, provenance, and stable launch session keys.
- Added `LaunchRoutingService` and threaded it through onboarding, setup handoff, gateway bootstrap, Ops Mesh draft generation, and mission creation instead of leaving lane choice spread across ad hoc heuristics.
- Gateway bootstrap state now persists routing policy and continuity hints: `route_binding_mode`, `last_route_instance_id`, and `last_route_resolved_at`.
- Mission drafts and stored missions now carry a durable `session_key`, so repeated launches can keep a stable logical routing identity even when runtime thread ids change.
- Remote-first launches now prefer the last healthy workspace lane, otherwise a connected lane already attached to the saved workspace, before falling back to a generic connected lane.
- The dashboard/setup UI now renders the resolved launch route directly: route mode, match provenance, session key, warnings, and candidate lanes.

Primary files carrying this slice:

- `src/openzues/services/launch_routing.py`
- `src/openzues/services/gateway_bootstrap.py`
- `src/openzues/services/onboarding.py`
- `src/openzues/services/setup.py`
- `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py`
- `src/openzues/services/missions.py`
- `src/openzues/database.py`
- `src/openzues/schemas.py`
- `src/openzues/web/static/app.js`
- `src/openzues/web/static/app.css`
- `tests/test_app.py`
- `tests/test_database.py`

  - dashboard payload via `gateway_bootstrap`

## Update: Re-entrant Setup Posture + Scoped Reset

Date: 2026-04-11

### Completed this turn

- Closed the next onboarding parity seam from OpenClaw's setup flow: OpenZues now has an explicit setup posture service instead of a one-shot bootstrap path.
- Added durable setup footprint tracking so the product remembers which lane, workspace, operator, task, and optional first-run resources were created or reused during bootstrap.
- Added a new setup inspection surface that explains whether the operator should `bootstrap`, `keep`, `modify`, or `reset`, and it finishes with a concrete operational handoff instead of stopping at "saved".
- Added scoped reset behavior with OpenClaw-style intent:
  - `config`: clear the saved gateway bootstrap profile
  - `config+creds+sessions`: clear the profile, revoke the saved operator API key, clear saved approval/session requests, and disconnect the current lane
  - `full`: additionally remove bootstrap-managed resources when it is safe to do so, while preserving history-bound records when deletion would orphan audit state
- Added a lightweight CLI seam beside `openzues gateway bootstrap`:
  - `openzues setup`
  - `openzues setup bootstrap`
  - `openzues setup reset --scope ...`
- Fixed a CLI runtime bug while landing the slice: command invocations now hydrate `Settings()` per run, so `OPENZUES_DATA_DIR` overrides like `OPENZUES_DATA_DIR` work correctly in isolated environments.
- Tightened gateway bootstrap backfill so explicit setup resets stay reset. Legacy backfill still helps older databases that predate setup footprint metadata, but it no longer silently rehydrates a profile immediately after an intentional reset.

Primary files carrying this slice:

- `src/openzues/services/setup.py`
- `src/openzues/services/onboarding.py`
- `src/openzues/services/gateway_bootstrap.py`
- `src/openzues/cli.py`
- `src/openzues/app.py`
- `src/openzues/database.py`
- `src/openzues/schemas.py`
- `src/openzues/services/access.py`
- `src/openzues/services/manager.py`
- `tests/test_app.py`

## Update: Mode-Aware Setup Wizard Session

Date: 2026-04-11

### Completed this turn

- Closed the next setup parity seam from OpenClaw's wizard flow: OpenZues setup is now explicitly mode-aware (`local` vs `remote`) instead of treating QuickStart as a one-shot local-only post.
- Added a durable setup wizard session in SQLite so web QuickStart and terminal setup no longer behave like stateless form posts. The session remembers mode, flow, and the last non-secret bootstrap draft values.
- Added remote-first bootstrap behavior to onboarding:
  - remote mode no longer assumes OpenZues will create a Desktop lane
  - remote mode can stage workspace, operator access, and recurring tasking without pinning a default lane
  - when no lane exists yet, the result now returns a staged handoff instead of faking a launch-ready draft
- Extended the saved gateway bootstrap profile so it now records setup mode and flow, and its readiness logic reflects remote-first posture instead of only lane-centric local posture.
- Exposed the wizard session end to end:
  - `GET /api/setup/wizard`
  - `PUT /api/setup/wizard`
  - `openzues setup wizard`
  - `openzues setup wizard update --mode ... --flow ...`
- Updated the dashboard QuickStart shell to surface the saved setup posture, show the new mode and flow selectors, and switch behavior correctly when remote mode is selected.

## Recovery checkpoint 2026-04-12 21:42 America/Chicago

### Recovered context

- The previously identified outbound replay gap is still the live next seam; nothing in the current target workspace closes it yet.
- This turn stayed narrow on proof instead of starting the replay implementation branch.

### What is already true

- OpenZues persists outbound delivery rows with attempt metadata in `src/openzues/database.py`.
- OpenZues delivery execution in `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py` still creates each row as `pending` and immediately flips it to `delivered` or `failed` within the same synchronous webhook attempt.
- OpenClaw already carries a dedicated recovery path in `openclaw-main/src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue-recovery.ts` with explicit retry/backoff and `ackDelivery` / `failDelivery` handling.

### Concrete claim verified

OpenZues still lacks a recovery-safe outbound replay entrypoint; outbound deliveries are durable records, but not yet a reloadable retry queue.

Verification proof:

- `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py:3357-3457` shows `_deliver_notifications()` creating an outbound row with `delivery_state="pending"` and then updating that same row directly to `delivered` or `failed` after one webhook attempt.
- `src/openzues/database.py:1280-1412` shows only list/get/create/update helpers for outbound deliveries; there is no targeted queue reload, claim, ack, fail, or replay helper.
- `rg -n "replay|recover.*outbound|outbound.*recover|replay_outbound|ack_outbound|fail_outbound|retry_outbound" src/openzues tests` returned only parity-ledger mission text in `tests/test_missions.py`, with no OpenZues implementation hit for an outbound recovery path.
- `openclaw-main/src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue-recovery.ts:1-220` confirms the source product already has the missing class of behavior: recovery drain, retry backoff, permanent-error classification, and `ackDelivery` / `failDelivery` transitions.

### Operator handoff

Completed: landed the recovery turn by re-verifying the active outbound parity gap instead of reopening broad source inspection.

Verified: the next missing seam is still replay-safe outbound recovery; the target product does not yet expose an entrypoint that reloads and retries saved pending or failed deliveries.

Next smallest step: add the smallest OpenZues recovery seam on top of the existing outbox contract by introducing replay-eligible delivery queries/state transitions plus one operator trigger that reuses the current webhook sender.

## Update: Routed Outbox Ledger + Recovery Sweep Guardrails

Date: 2026-04-12

### Recovered context

- Re-entered from the 2026-04-12 recovery checkpoint instead of reopening the source inventory or widening the parity ledger search again.
- Verified that the current uncheckpointed worktree already held the next real additive seam: the first durable routed outbox ledger on top of `conversation_target` and `session_key`.
- Verified that the same worktree also carried a bounded recovery hardening slice in `missions.py` so stale-thread rebounds stop drifting into broad parity-ledger and session-artifact sweeps.

### Completed this turn

- Landed the first durable routed outbox ledger in OpenZues:
  - added `outbound_deliveries` persistence in SQLite
  - added `OutboundDeliveryView` plus `route_scope` schema coverage
  - notification-route tests and live routed deliveries now persist `session_key`, `conversation_target`, route match, delivery state, attempt counts, and error state
  - the dashboard Ops Mesh surface now shows recent routed delivery attempts instead of hiding that state inside route rows only

## Recovery checkpoint 2026-04-12 20:51 America/Chicago

### Recovered context

- The previous recovery checkpoint is now stale relative to the workspace: the routed send/outbox contract it described as missing is already present in OpenZues.
- This turn avoided another broad parity inventory pass and instead verified the currently shipped outbound-delivery seam directly in the target product.

### Concrete claim verified

OpenZues already has the first durable routed outbox contract across storage, service wiring, CLI, and dashboard surfaces.

Target proof:

- `src/openzues/database.py` already defines `outbound_deliveries`, indexes, plus create/list/get/update helpers for durable outbound records.
- `src/openzues/schemas.py` already defines `OutboundDeliveryView`, and `src/openzues/services/ops_mesh.py` already records pending deliveries, then marks them `delivered` or `failed` after webhook attempts.
- `src/openzues/cli.py`, `src/openzues/app.py`, and `src/openzues/web/static/app.js` already surface saved outbound deliveries through operator-facing CLI and dashboard flows.

Verification proof:

- `.\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_ops_mesh.py -k "outbound_delivery or notification_route" -q` -> `5 passed`
- `.\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_cli.py -k "routes_deliveries or routes_list_json_surfaces_saved_notification_routes" -q` -> `2 passed`
- `.\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_app.py -k "test_mission_creation_appears_on_dashboard or test_task_creation_roundtrips_conversation_target_into_dashboard_draft" -q` -> `2 passed`
- `node --check src/openzues/web/static/app.js` passed
- `.\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m compileall src/openzues` passed

### What remains

The remaining outbound parity gap is recovery-safe delivery replay, not outbox creation.

- OpenClaw persists queued outbound entries with replay-oriented lifecycle helpers such as `enqueueDelivery`, `ackDelivery`, `failDelivery`, and queue reload/recovery behavior.
- OpenZues currently records outbound attempts durably, but delivery handling is still synchronous per event/test route and stops at recording `pending`, `delivered`, or `failed`; there is no replay worker or recovery path that reloads and retries pending/failed outbound deliveries after interruption.

### Next best slice

Implement the smallest additive outbound recovery seam on top of the existing outbox contract:

- add explicit retry/replay lifecycle semantics for outbound deliveries instead of treating the saved row as a write-only audit log
- provide one recovery entrypoint that reloads eligible pending or failed deliveries and replays them through the existing webhook delivery path
- keep the slice contract-safe across database state transitions, service logic, CLI or operator trigger surface, and dashboard visibility in the same turn
- verify with focused outbound-delivery tests plus one broader app/dashboard surface before checkpointing

### Operator handoff

Completed: corrected the parity ledger to match the current workspace by proving the routed outbox contract is already implemented and verified in OpenZues.

Verified: outbound-delivery persistence and operator surfaces are live across database, service, CLI, and dashboard layers; the next missing seam is delivery recovery/replay semantics.

## Recovery checkpoint 2026-04-15 gateway bootstrap session-mapping resolution America/Chicago

  `.\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_method_policy.py tests/test_session_keys.py -q`

Verified:

- OpenClaw `src/gateway/boot.ts` still snapshots and restores a main-session mapping around the boot run because it dispatches through agent-session config helpers.
- OpenZues [src/openzues/services/gateway_bootstrap.py](/C:/Users/skull/OneDrive/Documents/OpenZues/src/openzues/services/gateway_bootstrap.py) launches startup boot with `self.manager.start_thread(...)`, extracts a concrete `thread_id`, and immediately calls `self.manager.start_turn(...)` against that thread.
- OpenZues [src/openzues/services/manager.py](/C:/Users/skull/OneDrive/Documents/OpenZues/src/openzues/services/manager.py) `start_thread(...)` already treats runtime thread identity as the durable primitive:
  it records pre-launch `known_thread_ids`, recovers a newly visible thread on timeout, upserts the returned `thread_id` into `runtime.threads`, appends it to `runtime.loaded_thread_ids`, and waits for thread visibility before returning.
- The focused parity checks remain green after that verification: `30 passed`.

Conclusion:

- Do not add an OpenClaw-style boot session-store snapshot/restore shim to `gateway_bootstrap.py`.
- On the OpenZues path, boot isolation is already handled by explicit thread creation and runtime thread tracking, so the earlier uncertainty was architectural, not an unfinished implementation gap.

## Recovery checkpoint 2026-04-16 dedicated browser operator CLI surface America/Chicago

- Added `openzues browser status`, backed by saved-launch and live gateway browser-runtime inventory so operators can inspect whether browser-led parity work is actually staged.

- Added `openzues browser open` and `openzues browser snapshot` so parity work can drive and inspect the browser session directly from the CLI instead of hiding all browser behavior behind `watch`.
- Reused the existing `_watch_browser_verify_guarded`, `_run_browser_command`, `gateway_bootstrap`, and `gateway_capability` seams instead of inventing a second browser subsystem.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 saved lane reconnect parity America/Chicago

- Landed the next truthful OpenClaw `nodes.ts` wake-adjacent seam without pretending OpenZues has APNS: `GatewayNodeMethodService` now accepts a wake callback, and `GatewayNodeService.wake_node()` uses the real saved-lane reconnect primitive `RuntimeManager.connect_instance(...)` for remembered OpenZues nodes whose ids map to managed instances.
- Refined `node.pending.enqueue` so `wakeTriggered` is no longer hard-coded to `false` for every lane. When the work item is newly queued, wake is allowed, and the target is a disconnected saved OpenZues lane, the method now attempts a reconnect and reports whether that reconnect actually restored the node session.
- Refined `node.invoke` so a remembered but disconnected saved lane gets one reconnect attempt before OpenZues gives up with `NOT_CONNECTED`, matching the OpenClaw control-flow shape more closely even though the OpenZues peer is manager reconnect rather than APNS.
- Tightened `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py` to run the node HTTP shim against the exact node registry seam without unrelated leader-only background loops; the gateway-node parity pack is now stable under strict warning handling instead of leaking nondeterministic `aiosqlite` teardown warnings.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_service.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_actions.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_work.py`

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 node pair list parity America/Chicago

- Landed the smallest truthful `node.pair.*` seam from OpenClaw `nodes.ts`: `GatewayNodeMethodService` now supports `node.pair.list` and returns the OpenClaw-shaped `{ pending, paired }` contract.
- The current OpenZues peer is intentionally bounded: `pending` stays empty because there is still no persisted pairing request state machine, while `paired` is derived from the real remembered/known node roster and shaped with pairing-safe fields (`nodeId`, display name/platform/version metadata, remote IP, permissions, approved timestamp, and last connected timestamp).
- This gives the existing HTTP shim at `POST /api/gateway/node-methods/call` a truthful pairing roster surface, which is enough for OpenClaw-style callers that fall back from `node.list` to `node.pair.list` when they only need a paired-node inventory.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 node pair request and reject parity America/Chicago

- Landed the first persisted `node.pair.*` lifecycle in OpenZues using the existing SQLite `Database`: `node.pair.request`, `node.pair.list`, and `node.pair.reject` now share a real pending-pairing store instead of a stub.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now accepts the OpenClaw-shaped request payload (`nodeId`, display metadata, `caps`, `commands`, `remoteIp`, `silent`) and returns the OpenClaw-shaped `{ status: "pending", request, created }` response. Repeated requests for the same node refresh the existing request instead of minting duplicates.
- `node.pair.list` now surfaces both sides truthfully: pending requests come from SQLite with derived `requiredApproveScopes`, while paired nodes still come from the real remembered node roster already wired through `GatewayNodeService`.
- `node.pair.reject` now removes a persisted pending request and returns the OpenClaw-shaped `{ requestId, nodeId }` payload.
- The pending approval-scope mapping matches the current OpenClaw contract: no commands -> `operator.pairing`; declared non-system commands -> `operator.pairing` + `operator.write`; `system.run` / `system.run.prepare` -> `operator.pairing` + `operator.admin`.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_pairing.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/database.py`

  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/database.py src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_pairing.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_actions.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_work.py`
- Result: mypy passed on the touched pairing modules, `55 passed` in the gateway-node pack, and Ruff passed.
- Remaining honest blockers after this pairing lifecycle slice:
  - `node.pair.approve` is still unported because OpenZues does not yet persist a paired-node token roster for arbitrary non-manager nodes.
  - `node.pair.verify` depends on that same token-bearing paired-node store.
  - `node.rename` still needs a truthful rename target for non-manager paired nodes.
  - `node.event` still lacks an OpenZues `server-node-events` runtime.
  - `node.canvas.capability.refresh` still lacks a real canvas host / scoped capability token runtime.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 node approve, verify, rename, and catalog parity America/Chicago

- Extended the new SQLite-backed node pairing store into a full approved-node lifecycle: `node.pair.approve`, `node.pair.verify`, and `node.rename` now work against a persisted paired-node roster with stable `createdAtMs`, refreshed `approvedAtMs`, and per-node pairing tokens.
- `node.pair.approve` now promotes a pending request into the paired roster, rotates a fresh 256-bit base64url token, preserves the original creation timestamp on re-approval, and enforces OpenClaw-style scope requirements when explicit caller scopes are present (`operator.pairing`; plus `operator.write` for non-exec commands; plus `operator.admin` for `system.run` / `system.run.prepare`).
- `node.pair.verify` now checks the stored token and returns the approved node payload on success, while `node.rename` updates the persisted paired-node display name.
- Approved non-manager nodes are now visible through the main node catalog too: `node.list`, `node.describe`, `GET /api/gateway/nodes`, and `GET /api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}` merge the persisted paired roster with the live manager-backed registry so a newly approved node can be rediscovered through normal inventory endpoints after restart.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 remote gateway node method auth and scope threading America/Chicago

- Closed the next honest parity gap in the local HTTP gateway-node shim: non-loopback callers hitting `/api/gateway/node-methods/call` now have to authenticate with an operator API key instead of being treated like an unscoped local caller.
- Added `resolve_gateway_method_scopes_for_role(...)` in `src/openzues/services/access.py` so OpenZues can expand remote operator roles into a stable gateway scope set before calling the node-method service: `viewer` -> `operator.read`; `operator` -> `operator.read` + `operator.write` + `operator.approvals` + `operator.pairing`; `admin` / `owner` -> the same plus `operator.admin` and `operator.talk.secrets`.
- Wired `/api/gateway/node-methods/call` to build a real `GatewayNodeMethodRequester` for remote clients using `require_remote_operator(..., "dashboard.read")`, then pass those role-derived gateway scopes into `GatewayNodeMethodService.call(...)`.
- Verified the new boundary end-to-end: a remote client without an API key is now rejected, a remote `operator` can no longer approve a `system.run` pairing request because it is missing `operator.admin`, and a remote `owner` can approve the same request successfully.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "remote_gateway_node_method_call_requires_api_key or remote_gateway_node_pair_approve_threads_operator_gateway_scopes" -q -W error`

  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_app.py -k "remote_management_requires_admin_auth or remote_owner_can_manage_teams_with_api_key or remote_mission_endpoint_requires_api_key" -q -W error`
- Result: the focused remote auth tests passed, Ruff passed, the widened gateway-node + remote access pack passed (`65 passed`), and the extra remote app auth checks passed (`3 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this HTTP auth + scope slice:
  - `/api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}/method-call` is still a local-trust path and does not yet carry a truthful remote node-auth/token contract.
  - `node.event` still lacks an OpenZues `server-node-events` runtime.
  - `node.canvas.capability.refresh` still lacks a real canvas host / scoped capability token runtime.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 remote node-scoped method token auth America/Chicago

- Closed the next local-trust gap in the gateway-node HTTP surface: non-loopback callers hitting `/api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}/method-call` now have to prove the paired node identity with the persisted pairing token instead of being trusted purely by the path `node_id`.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 generic node.event ingress adapter America/Chicago

- Replaced the hard `503` stub for `node.event` with the first truthful OpenZues runtime peer: when the node-method service is wired with the existing database + broadcast hub, `node.event` now records a node-origin event into the `events` table and broadcasts a `node_event` envelope to hub subscribers.
- The bounded contract mirrors the OpenClaw handler shape where it matters for ingress: `event` is required, `payloadJSON` may be supplied directly, and when only `payload` is supplied OpenZues now serializes it into `payloadJSON` before persistence. The stored event record carries `nodeId`, `event`, `payloadJSON`, and the original `payload` when present.
- The app constructor now passes the live SQLite database and `BroadcastHub` into `GatewayNodeMethodService`, so the real HTTP path can exercise this runtime instead of falling back to the explicit unavailable stub.
- Verified end-to-end that a remote paired node can send `node.event` through `/api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}/method-call` using its pairing token and that the event is durably written into the shared event log.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "node_event_records_event_and_broadcasts_when_runtime_wired" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "remote_node_event_endpoint_records_event" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/access.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_actions.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_work.py tests/test_remote_ops.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_app.py -k "remote_management_requires_admin_auth or remote_owner_can_manage_teams_with_api_key or remote_mission_endpoint_requires_api_key" -q -W error`
- Result: Ruff passed, the touched modules type-checked cleanly, the widened gateway-node + remote access pack passed (`69 passed`), and the extra remote app auth checks stayed green (`3 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this node-event ingress slice:
  - This is still only a generic ingress adapter, not the full OpenClaw `server-node-events` runtime. Voice, APNS, outbound session routing, heartbeat nudges, and other rich side effects are still unported.
  - `node.canvas.capability.refresh` still lacks a real canvas host / scoped capability token runtime.
  - APNS/mobile wake, throttle, and nudge behavior still have no truthful OpenZues peer.
  - Full-file `mypy` on `src/openzues/app.py` still reports unrelated preexisting typing debt outside the slices landed here.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 bounded node.canvas.capability.refresh parity America/Chicago

- Ported the bounded OpenClaw `node.canvas.capability.refresh` contract into OpenZues for live node sessions that publish a base canvas host URL. The node registry now carries `canvas_host_url` plus the refreshed capability token/expiry on each connected node session.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now mirrors the source handler's essential behavior for this seam: if the connected node session has no base canvas host URL it still returns the truthful `canvas host unavailable for this node session` error, but when a host URL is present it mints a fresh scoped capability token, stores the token + expiry on the node session, and returns a scoped host URL built under `__openclaw__/cap/<token>` with a 10-minute TTL.
- Verified the real node-scoped HTTP path end-to-end: a remote paired node with a valid pairing token can now call `/api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}/method-call` for `node.canvas.capability.refresh` and receive the scoped canvas capability payload.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "node_canvas_capability_refresh_rotates_scoped_host_url" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "remote_node_canvas_capability_refresh_returns_scoped_host_url or test_gateway_node_scoped_method_call_endpoint_surfaces_node_role_methods" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_registry.py src/openzues/services/access.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_actions.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_work.py tests/test_remote_ops.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_app.py -k "remote_management_requires_admin_auth or remote_owner_can_manage_teams_with_api_key or remote_mission_endpoint_requires_api_key" -q -W error`
- Result: Ruff passed, the touched node modules type-checked cleanly, the widened gateway-node + remote access pack passed (`71 passed`), and the extra remote app auth checks stayed green (`3 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this bounded canvas slice:
  - This only covers the live session refresh contract. OpenZues still does not implement the larger canvas host serving/auth surface that consumes the minted capability, nor the richer surrounding OpenClaw UI host runtime.
  - The full OpenClaw `server-node-events` runtime side effects remain unported; OpenZues only has the new generic node-event ingress adapter so far.
  - APNS/mobile wake, throttle, and nudge behavior still have no truthful OpenZues peer.
  - Full-file `mypy` on `src/openzues/app.py` still reports unrelated preexisting typing debt outside the slices landed here.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 pending-work API wake threading America/Chicago

- Closed the next contract gap between the higher-level REST surface and the already-landed node RPC behavior: `/api/gateway/nodes/{node_id}/pending-work` now accepts the same bounded `wake` flag and threads it all the way into `GatewayNodeService.enqueue_pending_work`.
- Before this slice, the public pending-work API silently discarded `wake: true` and always returned `wakeTriggered: false`, even when the underlying managed lane could truthfully reconnect. OpenZues now reuses its existing managed-lane wake path so the REST contract reports a real wake attempt instead of a hardcoded stub.
- The wake behavior remains intentionally narrow and honest: OpenZues can only wake managed lanes it knows how to reconnect through `RuntimeManager.connect_instance`; it still does not claim OpenClaw's APNS/mobile wake, retry, throttle, or nudge runtime for paired mobile nodes.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "pending_work_endpoint_can_request_wake_for_managed_lane" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/schemas.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_service.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_service.py src/openzues/schemas.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -q -W error`
- Result: the new API wake test passed, Ruff passed, the touched service/schema modules type-checked cleanly, and the focused gateway-node API + method pack stayed green (`41 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this pending-work wake slice:
  - This only exposes wake truthfully for managed reconnectable lanes. OpenClaw's APNS/mobile wake, retry, throttle, and nudge behavior still have no truthful OpenZues peer.
  - The full OpenClaw `server-node-events` runtime side effects remain unported; OpenZues only has the generic node-event ingress adapter so far.
  - OpenZues still does not implement the larger canvas host serving/auth surface that consumes the minted canvas capability token.
  - Full-file `mypy` on `src/openzues/app.py` still reports unrelated preexisting typing debt outside the slices landed here.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 voicewake gateway method parity America/Chicago

- Closed another policy-only seam by landing a bounded runtime peer for OpenClaw's `voicewake.get` and `voicewake.set` methods. OpenZues now persists voice wake triggers under the control-plane data directory and serves them back through the gateway method dispatcher instead of advertising method names it could not execute.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_voicewake.py` as a small file-backed config owner that mirrors the important OpenClaw behavior for this slice: default triggers (`openclaw`, `claude`, `computer`), whitespace trimming, empty-input fallback to defaults, a bounded saved config file, and persisted `updatedAtMs` state.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now supports `voicewake.get` and `voicewake.set`, broadcasts `voicewake.changed` over the existing hub, and fans the same event out to any currently connected nodes so the runtime can react without pretending OpenZues already has OpenClaw's full websocket session runtime.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "voicewake" tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "voicewake" -q -W error`

  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_voicewake.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_registry.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_actions.py tests/test_gateway_node_pending_work.py -q -W error`
- Result: the new voicewake method tests passed, Ruff passed, the touched modules type-checked cleanly, and the widened gateway-node regression pack stayed green (`71 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this voicewake slice:
  - OpenZues still does not implement OpenClaw's richer websocket/session runtime around node connect-time voicewake snapshots; this slice only covers persisted get/set plus bounded change fanout.
  - APNS/mobile wake, retry, throttle, and nudge behavior still have no truthful OpenZues peer.
  - The full OpenClaw `server-node-events` runtime side effects remain unported; OpenZues only has the generic node-event ingress adapter so far.
  - OpenZues still does not implement the larger canvas host serving/auth surface that consumes the minted canvas capability token.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 skills.bins gateway method parity America/Chicago

- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_bins.py` as a small local collector that scans Codex-home and workspace skill roots, reads `SKILL.md` frontmatter, and extracts declared bins from the same metadata shapes OpenClaw uses for this method (`requires.bins`, `requires.anyBins`, and `install[].bins`).

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 managed voicewake connect snapshot parity America/Chicago

- Closed the remaining voicewake runtime seam around managed node connects. OpenZues now sends a bounded `voicewake.changed` snapshot when a managed lane first appears in the gateway registry or reconnects after a disconnect, instead of only supporting persisted `voicewake.get` / `voicewake.set` plus change fanout.
- `GatewayNodeService` now accepts the shared `GatewayVoiceWakeService`, detects fresh managed connections during `_sync()`, and pushes the current trigger snapshot through the existing node-event channel exactly once per connect cycle instead of on every background sync tick.
- `create_app(...)` now wires the same voicewake config service into both the gateway method dispatcher and the managed node sync service, so saved trigger updates and connect-time snapshots stay on one truthful source of state.
- Added coverage in `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py` for both layers:
  - a service-level proof that a managed node gets one `voicewake.changed` snapshot on first sync, none on a steady-state sync, and one more after disconnect/reconnect
  - an app-wiring proof that the real app state shares the saved voicewake config between `voicewake.set` and managed-node sync delivery

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 local skills.status parity America/Chicago

- Landed a bounded runtime peer for OpenClaw's `skills.status` contract instead of leaving it advertised without an implementation. OpenZues now returns a truthful local skill inventory report built from discovered `SKILL.md` metadata under the same local surfaces it already scans for `skills.bins`.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_status.py` as a small read-only inventory builder. It reports:
  - `workspaceDir` from the active workspace root
  - `managedSkillsDir` from `CODEX_HOME/skills`
  - one `skills[]` entry per discovered local skill with `name`, `description`, `source`, `bundled`, `filePath`, `baseDir`, `skillKey`, `primaryEnv`, `homepage`, `always`, `disabled`, `blockedByAllowlist`, `eligible`, `requirements`, `missing`, `configChecks`, and `install`
- The status logic is intentionally narrow and honest: it checks local bin presence, environment-variable requirements, declared platform compatibility, and declared install metadata, but it does not pretend OpenZues already has OpenClaw's ClawHub search/detail/install/update stack, allowlist policy engine, or remote eligibility/runtime.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now serves `skills.status` through the gateway dispatcher alongside `skills.bins`, with optional `agentId` accepted but not yet used to partition the report.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "skills_status" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "skills_status" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_status.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_status.py`
- Result: both new `skills.status` proofs passed, Ruff passed, and the touched service files type-checked cleanly.
- Remaining honest blockers after this skills-status slice:
  - `skills.search` and `skills.detail` remain ClawHub-oriented in OpenClaw; OpenZues still has no truthful remote marketplace peer for them.
  - `skills.install` / `skills.update` remain unported beyond the read-only local inventory surface.
  - Agent-scoped skill partitioning is not implemented; OpenZues currently returns one workspace-local report regardless of `agentId`.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 node pairing event broadcast parity America/Chicago

- Closed the adjacent runtime seam around the node pairing lifecycle. OpenZues pairing methods now publish OpenClaw-shaped gateway events over the hub instead of mutating pairing state silently.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now emits:
  - `node.pair.requested` when a brand-new pending pairing request is created
  - `node.pair.resolved` with `{ requestId, nodeId, decision, ts }` when a pending request is approved or rejected
- The request path stays faithful to OpenClaw's intent by only broadcasting the pending request event on first creation, not on later refreshes of the same request id.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 local skills.search/detail plus gateway identity parity America/Chicago

- Landed a bounded local peer for OpenClaw's `skills.search` and `skills.detail` methods instead of leaving the newly advertised read surface partially implemented. OpenZues now scans the same local skill roots it already uses for `skills.bins` / `skills.status`, builds stable local slugs, and returns deterministic discovery/detail payloads for workspace and Codex-home skills.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_catalog.py` as a small read-only catalog service. It parses `SKILL.md` frontmatter, dedupes overlapping roots, returns OpenClaw-adjacent search hits (`score`, `slug`, `displayName`, `summary`, `version`, `updatedAt`), and exposes detail payloads with bounded local owner metadata instead of pretending ClawHub is wired.
- Landed `gateway.identity.get` as a truthful persisted gateway-device identity surface. OpenZues now creates and stores a stable Ed25519-backed gateway identity under `data_dir/settings/gateway-identity.json` and returns `{ id, publicKey }` through the gateway method dispatcher so relay/pairing-adjacent clients have a real device identity to read.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_identity.py` and wired it through `create_app(...)` into `GatewayNodeMethodService`, keeping the identity stable across repeated API calls and future restarts for the same data dir.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "skills_search_and_detail or gateway_identity_get" -q -W error`

  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_identity.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_skill_catalog.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -q -W error`
- Result: the new local skills-discovery proofs passed, the gateway-identity proofs passed, Ruff stayed clean, the touched service files type-checked cleanly, and the widened gateway node-method/API packs stayed green (`33 passed` and `26 passed` in separate stable runs).
- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `skills.install` and `skills.update` still do not have the real ClawHub installer/config runtime behind them.
  - The full OpenClaw relay/APNS, wake-nudge, and gateway-signing flow is still unported; `gateway.identity.get` now exposes a stable local identity, but not the larger relay runtime.
  - OpenZues still does not implement OpenClaw's richer `system-presence`, `last-heartbeat`, or channel/login helper surfaces in the gateway dispatcher.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-16 explicit skills.install/update method shims America/Chicago

- Closed the last obvious advertised-method gap in the current gateway skills surface by turning `skills.install` and `skills.update` into explicit, mode-aware `UNAVAILABLE` shims instead of generic `unsupported method` failures.
- `GatewayNodeMethodService` now validates the documented OpenClaw parameter shapes for:
  - `skills.install` ClawHub mode (`source: "clawhub"`, `slug`, optional `version` / `force`)
  - `skills.install` gateway-installer mode (`name`, `installId`, optional dangerous override / timeout)
  - `skills.update` ClawHub mode (`source: "clawhub"`, `slug` or `all`)
  - `skills.update` config mode (`skillKey`, optional `enabled`, `apiKey`, `env`)
- Those methods now fail with explicit `503` responses that tell the caller which runtime is still missing, which is much closer to OpenClaw's real method catalog behavior than a bare unsupported-method error.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "skills.install or skills.update" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "skills_install_update_unavailable" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_identity.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py src/openzues/app.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_identity.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -q -W error`
- Result: the install/update shim proofs passed, the full gateway node-method suite stayed green (`33 passed`), the full gateway node API suite stayed green (`26 passed`), Ruff passed, and the touched service modules type-checked cleanly.
- Remaining honest blockers after this shim slice:
  - OpenZues still does not execute real ClawHub installs, gateway-host installer actions, or `skills.entries.<skillKey>` config patch persistence.
  - The larger relay/APNS, wake-nudge, system-presence, and heartbeat-runtime surfaces remain unported.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 system-presence gateway snapshot parity America/Chicago

- Landed a truthful OpenClaw-shaped `system-presence` read surface instead of leaving the advertised method catalog ahead of the implementation. OpenZues now emits a bounded presence snapshot composed from the persisted gateway identity plus currently connected gateway-node registry entries.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_system_presence.py` as a small formatter service. It emits a stable self entry for the local gateway device and maps connected registry peers into `backend` or `node` presence records with the expected OpenClaw-style fields (`deviceId`, `instanceId`, `mode`, `reason`, `roles`, `scopes`, `ts`, plus node metadata like `ip`, `deviceFamily`, and `modelIdentifier` when available).
- Wired `GatewayNodeMethodService` to expose `system-presence` directly from the shared registry and persisted gateway identity. Offline remembered nodes are intentionally excluded so the surface reflects live presence instead of stale memory.

- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `last-heartbeat` is still advertised in the gateway method policy, but OpenZues does not yet expose a dedicated heartbeat snapshot surface with equally bounded semantics.
  - The larger relay/APNS, wake-nudge, gateway-signing, and login/channel helper surfaces are still unported.
  - `skills.install` and `skills.update` now fail honestly, but the real ClawHub installer/config runtime is still missing.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 last-heartbeat and health gateway read parity America/Chicago

- Landed `last-heartbeat` as a real gateway read surface instead of another advertised-but-unsupported method. OpenZues now reads the latest persisted `node.event` heartbeat from SQLite and returns a bounded snapshot with `nodeId`, live node metadata when available, the recorded payload, the serialized payload JSON, and the persisted receive timestamp.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_last_heartbeat.py` plus a small `Database.get_latest_node_event(...)` helper so the heartbeat view stays restart-safe and reflects recorded gateway activity instead of in-memory guesses.
- Landed `health` as a native gateway method backed by the same control-plane role, owner PID, lock path, and runtime-update snapshot that already power `/api/health`. This keeps the gateway method catalog aligned with an existing truthful health source instead of leaving `health` policy-only.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_health.py` and wired it through `create_app(...)` into `GatewayNodeMethodService` so the method response stays aligned with the FastAPI health endpoint.
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k "last_heartbeat or health_method_surfaces" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k "last_heartbeat or supports_health" -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/app.py src/openzues/database.py src/openzues/services/gateway_health.py src/openzues/services/gateway_last_heartbeat.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_system_presence.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_health.py src/openzues/services/gateway_last_heartbeat.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/services/gateway_system_presence.py`

  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -q -W error`

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 talk.config bounded gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `talk.config` as a real gateway read method instead of leaving it in the same advertised-but-unsupported bucket as the deeper session runtime seams. OpenZues now validates the OpenClaw-style `includeSecrets` param, enforces the same `operator.talk.secrets` gate for remote callers, and returns a bounded talk-config snapshot through the gateway dispatcher.
- Added `src/openzues/services/gateway_talk_config.py` as a small formatter that is intentionally honest about current OpenZues ownership. Today it only emits sections OpenZues can truthfully source, so the default payload is `{"config": {}}` instead of pretending there is already a persisted talk/TTS provider stack, session main-key config, or UI seam-color contract in this repo.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 commands.list and tools.effective gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `tools.effective` as an explicit routed method instead of leaving it to fall through as an unsupported gateway call. OpenZues now validates the required `sessionKey`, accepts the bounded optional `agentId`, and returns a truthful `503 UNAVAILABLE` response that tells the operator the effective runtime tool inventory is not wired yet.
- Landed `commands.list` as a real bounded gateway read surface for the native OpenZues operator CLI. The new `src/openzues/services/gateway_commands.py` service publishes a stable native-only command inventory for the actual local control-plane commands that exist today, including browser, gateway, setup, Hermes, routes, and update entrypoints.
- Kept the contract intentionally honest:
  - all surfaced command entries are `source: native` and `scope: native` because OpenZues does not yet own OpenClaw's text-command, skill-command, or plugin-command runtime layers
  - `scope=text` returns an empty catalog instead of pretending text aliases exist
  - unknown `agentId` values now fail with the same bounded validation path instead of degrading into `unsupported method`
  - `includeArgs=false` suppresses the optional surfaced argument metadata while preserving the command inventory itself
- Added focused regression proof in both gateway unit and API suites for:
  - `tools.effective` requiring `sessionKey` and failing explicitly with `503`
  - `commands.list` returning the bounded native catalog
  - `commands.list` honoring scope and unknown-agent validation
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_commands.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_commands.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -q -W error`
  - `./.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -q -W error`
- Result: Ruff stayed clean, the touched gateway services type-checked cleanly, and the widened gateway node-method/API suites stayed green (`58 passed` and `44 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `chat.history`, `sessions.*`, `cron.*`, and `agents.files.*` are still policy-advertised without gateway dispatcher implementations.
  - `commands.list` is intentionally bounded to the native OpenZues CLI and does not yet mirror OpenClaw's richer text-command, skill-command, provider-native alias, or plugin-command runtime.
  - `tools.effective` now fails honestly, but OpenZues still does not expose a session-scoped effective-tool runtime view.
### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 status gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `status` as a real gateway read method by reusing the existing cached `build_status()` pipeline instead of cloning status assembly inside the gateway dispatcher. The gateway node-method endpoint now returns the same operator status shape already served from `/api/status`.
- Added an injected async status loader to `GatewayNodeMethodService` and wired it from `create_app(...)`, so the gateway method and the REST status route stay on one source of truth.
- Hardened `CodexDesktopService.discover()` so inaccessible Codex Desktop paths no longer crash status assembly. Permission-denied reads on session logs, package probes, session executable probes, and staged-runtime existence checks now degrade to an empty/not-ready desktop discovery snapshot instead of aborting the whole status surface.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 chat.history bounded gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `chat.history` as a real gateway read surface by projecting the persisted OpenZues control-chat transcript into the OpenClaw-shaped `messages` payload instead of leaving the method to fall through as `unsupported method`.
- Kept the slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - OpenZues currently serves the singleton persisted operator control-chat transcript, not a full OpenClaw multi-session store.
  - the gateway method echoes the requested `sessionKey`, returns `sessionId: null`, and leaves `thinkingLevel`, `fastMode`, and `verboseLevel` as `null` because those session-runtime fields are not owned by OpenZues yet.
  - `limit` is honored with the same `1..1000` request bounds as OpenClaw, and `maxChars` now applies a simple latest-first tail character budget over the projected text blocks so the endpoint has a truthful bounded read path instead of silently ignoring the parameter.
- Added focused regression proof in:
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_chat_history_returns_openclaw_shaped_control_chat_projection`
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_chat_history_applies_tail_char_budget_to_latest_message`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_supports_chat_history`
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`
  - `$env:PYTHONPATH=''C:\msys64\usr\lib\python3.12;.venv\Lib\site-packages;src''; & ''C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\Nsight Systems 2025.6.3\host-windows-x64\python\bin\python.exe'' -m mypy src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py`
  - `$env:PYTHONPATH=''C:\msys64\usr\lib\python3.12;.venv\Lib\site-packages;src''; & ''C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\Nsight Systems 2025.6.3\host-windows-x64\python\bin\python.exe'' -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k chat_history tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k chat_history`
- Result: Ruff stayed clean, the touched gateway dispatcher type-checked cleanly, and the focused gateway unit/API proofs all passed (`3 passed`).
- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `chat.send`, `sessions.*`, `cron.*`, and `agents.files.*` are still policy-advertised without dispatcher implementations.
  - `chat.history` is now executable, but it is intentionally a bounded projection of the singleton operator control-chat transcript rather than a full OpenClaw session-store replay.
- Next exact step: write failing gateway unit/API tests for `chat.send`, route it through the existing `/api/control-chat` submit path with OpenClaw-style param validation (`sessionKey`, `message`, `idempotencyKey`, optional `thinking`/`deliver`), and only then decide how much of the `runId`/abort contract OpenZues can truthfully acknowledge in this bounded runtime.
### Recovery addendum 2026-04-17 chat.send bounded gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `chat.send` as a real gateway write surface by validating the mobile/client request shape and routing it through the existing OpenZues control-chat submit path instead of leaving the method to fall through as `unsupported method`.
- Kept the slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - OpenZues now accepts the core OpenClaw client payload used by local chat transports: `sessionKey`, `message`, `idempotencyKey`, plus optional `thinking`, `deliver`, and `timeoutMs`.
  - the gateway bridge currently ignores `sessionKey`, `thinking`, `deliver`, and `timeoutMs` at execution time because OpenZues still owns a singleton operator control-chat runtime rather than a full session-scoped streaming chat engine.
  - the response is a small truthful acknowledgement, `{ "runId": <idempotencyKey>, "status": "ok" }`, because the bridged control-chat request completes synchronously and does not yet expose OpenClaw-style live run streaming.
- Added focused regression proof in:
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_chat_send_returns_run_ack_from_injected_control_chat_bridge`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_supports_chat_send`
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py src/openzues/app.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`

  - `$env:PYTHONPATH='C:\msys64\usr\lib\python3.12;.venv\Lib\site-packages;src'; & 'C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\Nsight Systems 2025.6.3\host-windows-x64\python\bin\python.exe' -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k 'chat_history or chat_send' tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k 'chat_history or chat_send'`
- Result: Ruff stayed clean, the touched gateway dispatcher type-checked cleanly, and the focused gateway unit/API proofs all passed (`5 passed`). The API proof also confirmed the gateway write path really persists through the existing control-chat runtime instead of a test-only stub.
- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `sessions.*`, `chat.abort`, `cron.*`, and `agents.files.*` are still policy-advertised without dispatcher implementations.
  - `chat.send` now acknowledges and persists text prompts, but it still does not expose OpenClaw's live run streaming, attachment ingestion, or abortable run state.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 sessions.list bounded gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `sessions.list` as a real gateway read surface instead of leaving OpenClaw mobile/chat clients to guess a session key or fall through to `unsupported method`.
- Kept the slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - OpenZues now exposes a singleton operator control-chat session inventory backed by existing SQLite state rather than pretending it already owns a full multi-session OpenClaw store.
  - the new `src/openzues/services/gateway_sessions.py` formatter derives a canonical main session key from saved gateway bootstrap routing state and reuses persisted mission session keys when a reusable `:thread:` child session already exists.
  - `defaults.mainSessionKey` always points at the canonical launch/main session key, while the surfaced session entry points at the currently reusable thread session when one is available.
  - `includeGlobal=false` now hides the singleton entry only when the current surfaced session is still the global/main lane; if OpenZues is already reusing a thread child session, the entry remains visible because it is no longer just the global lane.
  - `includeUnknown` is accepted for contract parity but remains a no-op because OpenZues does not yet persist unknown/external session rows.
- Added focused regression proof in:
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_sessions_list_returns_bounded_singleton_control_chat_inventory`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_supports_sessions_list`
- Verified with:
  - `./.venv/Scripts/ruff.exe check src/openzues/services/gateway_sessions.py src/openzues/services/gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py`

  - `$env:PYTHONPATH='C:\msys64\usr\lib\python3.12;.venv\Lib\site-packages;src'; & 'C:\Program Files\NVIDIA Corporation\Nsight Systems 2025.6.3\host-windows-x64\python\bin\python.exe' -m pytest tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py -k 'chat_history or chat_send or sessions_list' tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py -k 'chat_history or chat_send or sessions_list' -q -W error -W ignore::pytest.PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning -W ignore::ResourceWarning`
- Result: Ruff stayed clean, the touched gateway services type-checked cleanly, and the focused gateway unit/API parity proofs for `chat.history`, `chat.send`, and `sessions.list` all passed (`7 passed`). The wider full-module gateway suites remain noisy on this Windows workspace because pytest temp-directory and `TestClient` teardown warnings escape during cleanup, but the touched seam itself is green.
- Remaining honest blockers after this slice:
  - `chat.abort`, broader `sessions.*`, `cron.*`, and `agents.files.*` are still policy-advertised without dispatcher implementations.
  - `sessions.list` is now executable, but it is intentionally a bounded singleton control-chat inventory rather than a full OpenClaw session database with subscription, preview, patch, or delete flows.
- Next exact step: write failing gateway unit/API tests for `chat.abort`, then decide whether OpenZues can truthfully route that surface to a bounded control-chat cancellation path or should land it first as an explicit `503 UNAVAILABLE` contract instead of `unsupported method`.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 chat.abort explicit gateway parity America/Chicago

- Landed `chat.abort` as an explicit routed gateway method instead of letting OpenClaw clients fall through to `unsupported method` when they try to cancel a run.
- Kept the slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - OpenZues now validates the OpenClaw-shaped request surface, `sessionKey` plus optional `runId`, so bad requests fail on the real parameter contract instead of on a missing dispatcher branch.
  - the routed method currently returns `503 UNAVAILABLE` with a precise cancellation-runtime message because OpenZues still does not persist active control-chat run ids or own abort-controller state for the singleton control-chat lane.
  - this avoids pretending that the existing synchronous `chat.send` bridge can cancel or stream live runs before the underlying runtime exists.
- Added focused regression proof in:
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_chat_abort_fails_as_explicit_unavailable_until_runtime_is_wired`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_chat_abort_fails_explicitly_when_unwired`

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 cron.list plus agents.files read parity America/Chicago

- Landed `cron.list` as a real routed gateway read surface by projecting OpenZues scheduled task blueprints into the OpenClaw-shaped cron page contract instead of leaving the method to fall through as `unsupported method`.
- Kept the cron slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - only cadence-backed task blueprints are exposed right now, because that is the truthful scheduler inventory OpenZues already persists in SQLite.
  - the bridge accepts bounded OpenClaw-style read params, `enabled`, `includeDisabled`, `query`, `limit`, `offset`, `sortBy`, and `sortDir`, and returns the expected page envelope, `jobs`, `total`, `offset`, `limit`, `hasMore`, and `nextOffset`.
  - job state is derived from existing task-blueprint payload facts such as `last_launched_at`, `last_status`, and `last_result_summary`; OpenZues still does not claim OpenClaw's richer cron add/update/remove/run/runs runtime in this slice.
- Landed `agents.files.list` and `agents.files.get` as bounded workspace instruction reads instead of advertising agent-file methods with no executable owner:
  - added `src/openzues/services/gateway_agent_files.py` as a small workspace reader that exposes only the real OpenZues instruction surfaces, `AGENTS.md` and `.codex/AGENTS.md`.
  - the bridge accepts `main` or `openzues` as the requested agent id but returns a canonical singleton `main` agent view, because OpenZues does not yet own a full multi-agent workspace registry.
  - missing files are reported truthfully with `missing: true`, and reads stay allowlisted to the two instruction files above instead of pretending OpenZues can safely browse or mutate arbitrary agent workspace paths.
- Added focused regression proof in:
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_cron_list_returns_paged_scheduled_task_inventory`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_supports_cron_list`
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_agents_files_list_returns_bounded_workspace_instruction_inventory`
  - `tests/test_gateway_node_methods.py::test_agents_files_get_returns_workspace_instruction_file_content`
  - `tests/test_gateway_nodes_api.py::test_gateway_node_method_call_endpoint_supports_agents_files_list_and_get`

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 agents.list plus agent.identity.get plus cron.status parity America/Chicago

- Landed `agents.list` and `agent.identity.get` as real routed gateway methods instead of leaving OpenClaw clients to fall through to `unsupported method` when discovering the singleton assistant surface.
- Kept the agent slice intentionally honest and bounded:

  - `agent.identity.get` accepts either `main` or `openzues` as the requested alias, treats non-agent session keys as the singleton `main` lane, rejects malformed `agent:` session keys, and still refuses unknown multi-agent ids instead of pretending OpenZues already owns OpenClaw's full agent registry.
- Landed `cron.status` as a real routed scheduler summary instead of another advertised read hole:
  - the existing `src/openzues/services/gateway_cron.py` bridge now reports OpenClaw-shaped scheduler status from persisted OpenZues task-blueprint rows.
  - `storePath` points at the real OpenZues SQLite store, `jobs` counts only cadence-backed scheduled rows, and `nextWakeAtMs` is derived from the nearest enabled cadence task with a computable next run.
  - the status remains intentionally bounded: OpenZues still does not claim OpenClaw's full cron mutation, run-log, or wake runtime from this slice alone.

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 wake explicit gateway parity America/Chicago

### Recovery addendum 2026-04-18 cron.runs mission-backed read parity America/Chicago

- Landed `cron.runs` as a real routed gateway read seam instead of leaving advertised scheduled-run history to fall through as `unsupported method`.
- Kept the slice intentionally honest and bounded:
  - OpenZues now validates the OpenClaw-shaped request surface for `scope`, `id` or `jobId`, paging, status filters, delivery-status filters, query, and sort direction.
  - the run history is projected from current cadence-backed task blueprints plus finished scheduled missions already persisted in SQLite, using `missions.task_blueprint_id` as the source of truth rather than inventing a separate run-log runtime.
  - only finished scheduled missions are exposed right now, mapping `completed -> ok` and `failed -> error`; OpenZues does not yet claim OpenClaw's richer JSONL-backed run log, skipped-run tracking, or deleted-job history retention.
  - `scope: "all"` adds `jobName` from the live scheduled task inventory so query and overview views stay useful, while job-scoped reads stay tied to the same `task-blueprint:<id>` identifiers returned by `cron.list`.
